topic: PATCH-322-A verification: §2 self-contradiction cross-reference added to detail_reasoning
our_argument_summary: PRED-106 (16 Hz Ca²⁺ ICR bioelectromagnetics) was verdicted silent_ignore on 2026-05-08 after 36 byte-identical predictions.html polls past window-close (2026-04-30). PATCH-322-A (EXP-322, ISS-1814) extended detail_reasoning by ~140 words after the existing phantom-WIN-071 sentence to link that finding to a broader internal-consistency drift pattern, citing §2 (Self-Contradictions) — specifically §2.9 V13 self-referential loop, retrospective parameter fitting, and version-note retuning V51.0 → V51.1.

patch_under_review.reason_in_queue: PATCH-322-A: detail_reasoning extended with §2 self-contradiction pattern cross-reference (EXP-322, ISS-1814). Curmudgeon: verify prose flows and §2.9 reference resolves correctly.

verification_results.section_2_title_check: part2 title is "Part 2: Self-Contradictions — Does the Dome's Geometry Produce Its Claimed Predictions?" (sections.json key part2) and the in-HTML h2 anchor is "Self-Contradictions — The Dome's Own Geometry Refutes Its Claims" — the §2 (Self-Contradictions) reference resolves.
verification_results.section_2_9_anchor_check: h2 "2.9 The V13 Coordinate System: A Self-Referential Loop" present in part2.html (found at offset 23993). TLDR confirms: "The V13 Finsler coordinate system claims to predict inter-city distances on the flat disc, but the input coordinates are derived from globe distances." Detail confirms the closed-loop framing: "converts globe positions to dome coordinates, computes a predicted distance, and compares it against the globe distance it started from." Patch text "§2.9 takes globe distances as input and outputs predicted distances back to those same inputs (a closed loop, not a prediction)" matches §2.9 content cleanly.
verification_results.prose_flow: New text reads as a continuation of the existing sentence "...internal-consistency drift between the prediction registry and the wins registry." The bridge "This is one instance of a broader pattern documented in §2 (Self-Contradictions) — ..." lands grammatically and the closing sentence about "prospective claims and confirming evidence authored by the same agentic loop" returns to the same drift theme. No run-on, no orphan clause.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — this is a prose patch on a prediction record. The underlying claim (16 Hz Ca²⁺ ICR) is a bioelectromagnetics claim with no dome geometric derivation; the dome cites phantom WIN-071 as basis.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — patch did not touch code claims.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: PRED-106 is a bioelectromagnetics prediction with no API/monitor.py instrumentation — neither HARDCODED nor LIVE_FETCH, just a registered claim. The dome's monitor.py does not contain any Ca²⁺/ICR/16 Hz/bioEM domain. Unchanged by this patch.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: PRED-106 is closer to invoking a contested fringe literature (Liboff/Blackman/Adey ICR) than relabeling standard physics. The dome substitutes "ECM aetheric permeability" for the standard B-dependent ICR formula rather than renaming an established mechanism.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: PRED-106 was registered 2026-04-04 as prospective (window 2026-04-30). The patch makes no change to prospective/post-hoc status.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Cited basis is a phantom WIN-071 that does not exist in the dome's published wins.html (WIN-001 through WIN-069). No derivation from dome geometry. Patch does not change this; the phantom-citation finding is the patch's point of departure.

holes_found.description: The patch enumerates three §2 sub-patterns as "documented in §2 (Self-Contradictions)": (a) V13 self-referential loop, (b) WIN-by-WIN numerical agreements with parameters fitted retrospectively, (c) version notes V51.0 → V51.1 retuning parameters across releases. Only (a) is explicitly documented in §2 — §2.9 is the named anchor and matches cleanly. (b) is partially supported: §2 V51.0 subsection says cosmological WINs "retroactively claim" published results, and the same subsection notes coordinate WINs "curve-fit to globe data" — both close enough to count, though neither says "parameters fitted retrospectively" in those exact words. (c) is not documented in §2 at all. The only §2 subsection touching V51 is "The V51.0 Cosmological Expansion: Territory Without a Map," which is a critique of lack of derivation, not of V51.0→V51.1 cross-release retuning. Grep across part2.html shows zero occurrences of "V51.1," "retune," "retuned," or "version note." A dome defender will check the citation, find nothing in §2 about V51.0→V51.1, and call this an unsupported aside. The retuning claim is independently defensible from the dome's own evolution.html (V50.2 W004 -22.24 nT recalibration, V12 "V9 theta bug fixed," etc.) but that's a different citation than "§2 documents this."
holes_found.recommendation: Either (i) drop the V51.0 → V51.1 retuning clause from the enumeration so only V13 and retrospective fitting remain, both of which §2 actually covers; or (ii) add a parallel reference to the dome's own evolution.html (visible at raw-text/06-evolution.txt) which openly catalogues parameter changes across versions (V50.2 W004 -22.24 nT recalibration, V49.2 removal of circular reasoning, etc.) so the citation is real. Option (i) is the cheaper fix and preserves the patch's primary point (V13 + retrospective fitting are the strongest two pillars).

holes_found.description: CLASSIFICATION paragraph in detail_reasoning still reads "Assessment verdict: pending. After window closure, the decider should re-assign for a verdict update; expected outcomes are (i) silent drop … → silent_ignore (mirroring PRED-105 trajectory at 11+ days post-closure); (ii) PASS-mark via citing pre-existing Liboff/Blackman literature → recycled; (iii) genuine pre-registered replication." Window has been closed for 12 days; verdict moved to silent_ignore on 2026-05-08 (verdict_history entry); ISS-1886 already tracks d=14 tacit_withdrawal status. The prose still describes the outcome as future-tense and lists "≈ 1.5 days from this assessment" in the preceding sentence. Pre-existing staleness — NOT introduced by PATCH-322-A — but newly conspicuous because the surrounding paragraph was just edited. The notes field also enumerates outcome (iv) silent_SCRUB, which detail_reasoning omits; ISS-1768 and ISS-1779 are open and address exactly this rubric gap.
holes_found.recommendation: Separate cleanup pass (not blocking on this verification): convert CLASSIFICATION to past-tense around the actual silent_ignore outcome and reconcile with ISS-1768/ISS-1779 rubric additions. Could be folded into whichever EXP closes those open issues — do NOT graft onto PATCH-322-A retroactively.

holes_found.description: Three open issues read as near-duplicates of the now-closed ISS-1814 that PATCH-322-A addresses: ISS-1769 ("PRED-106 detail_reasoning does not connect phantom WIN-071 citation to the broader pattern of dome i…"), ISS-1780 ("detail_reasoning references WIN-071 as dome-cited phantom. Curmudgeon recommends audit"), and ISS-1794 ("PRED-106 detail_reasoning identifies phantom WIN-071 citation but does not connect it to the broader"). All three are status: assigned-analyst. The closing patch may subsume them in spirit but they are not flagged as closed.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should re-examine these three as candidates for closure-with-rationale (pointing at PATCH-322-A) or as still-distinct issues if they ask for something more than ISS-1814. Not blocking on this verification — just flagging the duplication so it does not linger.

stronger_arguments.description: The single sharpest cross-reference for the dome's registry-drift failure mode is not §2.9 V13 but the Timestamp Error tab itself (CLAUDE.md flag: "the canary"). The patch already says "prospective claims and confirming evidence are authored by the same agentic loop without an immutable separation between the two" — that is verbatim the Timestamp Error tab's thesis (OTS anchors observations, not predictions). Linking PRED-106's phantom-WIN-071 to the Timestamp Error finding (rather than only §2) would put the strongest single argument inline.
sources: project CLAUDE.md Timestamp Error tab description

kernel_of_truth.description: The patch correctly identifies that phantom-citation is structurally interesting, not merely a typo (EXP-322.json:kernel_of_truth). The dome is built by an agentic process (project CLAUDE.md: author confirmed via DM 2026-04-26) and the WIN registry / prediction registry are both author-authored, so the cited "WIN-071" being absent from the public registry is exactly the kind of fingerprint you would expect if registration and confirmation are not separated. The §2.9 V13 closed-loop case is also a real, well-documented self-contradiction (sections.json part2 §2.9: "The V13 Finsler coordinate system claims to predict inter-city distances on the flat disc, but the input coordinates are derived from globe distances").
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The hole here is not about the kernel — the kernel is sound. The hole is the citation accuracy of one of the three enumerated §2 sub-patterns (V51.0 → V51.1 retuning, EXP-322.json:patches[0].new_string). A reader who checks the citation will find §2.9 fully supports clause (a), partial support for clause (b), and no §2 coverage for clause (c). Tightening clause (c) does not weaken the patch — it strengthens it by removing an unsupported sub-claim.

rationale_for_recommended_action: The verification goal in the queue item ("verify prose flows and §2.9 reference resolves correctly") is met. §2.9 resolves; prose flows; verdict unchanged. The one moderate hole (H1) is a tightening recommendation, not a verdict-changing finding — H1 can be addressed by dropping seven words from the enumeration or by adding one external citation to evolution.html. H2 and H3 are pre-existing items surfaced by the verification but not introduced by this patch.
deeper_analysis: Counter-argument considered: a defender of PATCH-322-A could say the V51.0 → V51.1 retuning clause is implied by §2 because §2's V51.0 subsection critiques "WINs that do not derive from cavity equations" — i.e., V51.0 added five WINs without derivations, and V51.1 (current) inherits them. That is a charitable reading. The counter is that "WINs without derivations" is not the same claim as "V51.0 → V51.1 retunes parameters across releases." The first is a static absence-of-derivation claim about one version; the second is a dynamic cross-release retuning claim. The dome's evolution.html is the more honest cite for the second claim — and it is the dome's own page, which makes the citation harder to dismiss. Operator preference (user_preferences: "Think about the other side of the argument too") explicitly favors steelmanning, so flagging this asymmetry is the right call rather than letting an over-strong citation slip past. The other side: PATCH-322-A's author (analyst-mode1 batch 8) anticipated the "is it sourced?" objection in EXP-322 anticipated_objections and named §2.4, §2.6, §2.9 as supporting — those support clause (b) but not clause (c). The defense did not anticipate the clause (c) gap specifically.

The patch's framing "documented in §2 (Self-Contradictions) — ... dome version notes (V51.0 → V51.1) repeatedly retune parameters" is not supported by §2 itself. (H1.)
citations_unchecked: Bioelectromagnetics literature claims (Liboff 1985, Blackman 1985, Adey, Blanchard & Blackman 1994, Kirschvink thermal-noise critique) — unchecked in this verification run, were checked in PRED-batch-2026-04-29-PRED106.json cycle 1.
Whether PRED-105 truly mirrors silent_ignore trajectory at 11+ days post-closure — referenced as precedent but not re-checked here.

summary_for_decider: Verification PASSES on the queue-stated criteria (prose flows; §2.9 reference resolves). One moderate hole: the enumeration of three §2 sub-patterns includes one (V51.0 → V51.1 retuning) that §2 does not actually document — see H1 with two suggested fixes (drop the clause or add evolution.html citation). Two minor pre-existing items surfaced: stale CLASSIFICATION verdict-pending prose (H2) and three near-duplicate open issues that may be subsumed by PATCH-322-A (H3, ISS-1769/-1780/-1794). Recommended action minor_edit, not blocking. queue_id set to 341, matches queue item.
